name: Frozen Milky Way Mousse
recipeIngredient: Milky Way bars, cut into 1-inch pieces (1 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the sauce: Combine whipping cream, sugar, unsweetened cocoa powder, and unsalted butter in a heavy medium saucepan. Bring to simmer over medium-low heat, whisking constantly until the butter melts and sugar and cocoa dissolve. Remove from heat and whisk in vanilla extract and instant coffee powder. Cool sauce.
2. For the mousse: Line a 9x5-inch loaf pan with parchment paper.
3. Combine Milky Way pieces, semisweet chocolate, unsweetened chocolate, and unsalted butter in a large heatproof bowl set over a pan of simmering water. Stir constantly until melted (mixture will look grainy).
4. Remove the bowl from the hot water and let it cool slightly.
5. In a heavy small saucepan, combine sugar and water. Stir over low heat until the sugar dissolves. Increase the heat to high and boil the syrup until the temperature reaches 230°F on a candy thermometer.
6. Meanwhile, beat egg whites and cream of tartar in a bowl until soft peaks form. Gradually pour the boiling syrup into the egg whites, beating until stiff peaks form and the whites are cool (about 5 minutes).
7. Using a rubber spatula, fold the whites into the chocolate mixture in two additions.
8. In a medium bowl, beat chilled whipping cream and vanilla extract to soft peaks. Fold the cream into the chocolate mixture in two additions.
9. Spoon the mousse into the prepared pan. Cover with plastic wrap and freeze until firm (at least 6 hours).
10. Before serving, remove the plastic wrap from the top of the mousse. Invert it onto a platter. Remove the pan and parchment paper. Slice the mousse crosswise into 12 slices. Cut each slice diagonally into two triangles.
11. For each serving, arrange 2 mousse triangles on a plate. Spoon warm chocolate sauce over and around the mousse and serve.
